rulings.Case Title: HOWARD I. MAIBACH VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, A
rulings.Calendar Matter: Application For Leave To File Special Motion To Strike Pursuant To Ccp 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, November 22, 2024, line 1 RESPONDENT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S Application For Leave To File Special Motion To Strike Pursuant To Ccp 425.16 Off calendar. No party provided courtesy copies in compliance with SF Local Rule 2.7B. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work. = (302/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY LLC VS. CRYSTAL LEI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, November 22, 2024, line 6, DEFENDANT PETER POON AKA TING CHAM POON'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS Defendant Ting Cham Poon's motion to quash and vacate the default entered on March 14, 2023, is granted. The motion is timely. (CCP 473(d); California Capital Insurance Co. v. Hoehn (2024) DJDAR 10673 [void default judgment may be set aside at any time].) The motion is granted given: (1) the bad blood between the parties and defendant Poon's declaration denying service (Ting Cham Poon Declaration); (2) the policy to hear cases on the merits (McClain v. Kissler (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 399, 413); (3) process server Mora's lack of credibility in prior litigation (Fu Decl., Ex. A [Statement of Decision, CGC-18-572347]); (4) plaintiff's, Yan's, and counsel Disston's prior bad faith and frivolous conduct in litigation among the parties (Fu Decl., Ex. D [Court of Appeal Opinion, A167818]. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work. = (302/RCE)

The motion is granted without leave to amend as to the claim for attorney's fees as conceded. *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work. = (302/RCE)
